title: iberis ciliata subsp. balansae
intro: Iberis ciliata subsp. balansae, also known as the Alpine Candytuft, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and other bioactive substances. The health benefits of Iberis ciliata subsp. balansae include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
